what: real-Bedmap2 reordering of RTN by a hydraulic-routing connectivity phi(x,y)

anchor_constant_phi.note: reproduces committed baseline: Spearman -1, identical to thickness-above-flotation

verdict: constant-phi: Spearman(RTN, H_af/H)=-1.0000, 0 disagreeing cells (identical to thickness-above-flotation, as committed). hydraulic-routing phi(x,y): the GLOBAL Spearman stays -0.9998 (the continent is dominated by inland f=0 cells where RTN=0 for any phi), but in the near-flotation band it moves to -0.9880 (vs -1.0000 at constant phi); there phi_routed is nearly independent of the flotation fraction (Spearman -0.028), so it genuinely reorders the flotation signal -- the intrusion class CHANGES on 974 cells (139 newly flagged, 835 dropped), concentrated at the grounding line (median 10 vs 233 km). A geometry-only connectivity field already turns RTN into a different screen from plain flotation in the band that matters; whether the reordering is *correct* is the open test, needing a radar bed-specularity / basal-water phi (--specularity-npy) or a gridded intrusion survey to score against.
